summary: Verified locally against the canonical patch (single-file `setup_dkms_linked.py`, 86 additions) and the buggy baseline by sparse-cloning torvalds/linux at base commit bc47b2e823914966c15a09422f8fc3aa98d34c1b and running tests/test.sh. Discrimination is sharp: baseline (no script) scores 0.0000 via the hard gate at line 17 ('script does not exist -> exit 0'); a no-op stub that only mkdirs amdgpu-dkms/ scores 0.1000 (only the F2P-1 idempotency gate passes); a partial solution that writes dkms.conf+Makefile but no symlink tree scores 0.4500 (idempotent + dkms.conf + Makefile gates); the canonical patch scores 1.0000 (all 7 F2P gates pass on Linux paths — F2P-2 'symlink tree breadth+integrity' counts 2698 links with 0 broken/0 outside and 624 amdgpu targets; F2P-3 finds 5/6 essential sources; F2P-4..F2P-7 all pass). Note: on macOS-host one-shot validation, F2P-2/F2P-3 hardcoded prefix '/workspace/linux/' fails to match because /tmp resolves to /private/tmp via realpath — this is a macOS-only quirk. Inside Harbor's Linux Docker (where /workspace is the literal path with no symlink rewrite), the prefix matches and the canonical scores 1.0. All 7 F2P gates are behavioral and implementation-agnostic: F2P-2 accepts both `amdgpu-dkms/<sub>` and `amdgpu-dkms/amd/<sub>` layouts, F2P-1 supports both --force and idempotent re-run designs, F2P-4 reads dkms.conf via `bash -c 'source ...'` (real array semantics, no regex), F2P-5 checks for KDIR|kernel_source_dir|/lib/modules and M= (any of three KDIR conventions), F2P-6 stages via `cp -RL` and verifies amdgpu_drv.c content matches in-tree (DKMS-build path), F2P-7 verifies cwd-independent symlink resolution. Manifest weights sum to 1.0000; test.sh weights sum to 1.0000 (legacy `existing` is 0, so this is pure weighted-sum bounded to [0,1]). No P2P_REGRESSION declared — naturally bounded, no f2p_any_pass zeroing or additive-cap bug. Curator's prior FIX (16-file -> 1-file canonical strip on 2026-05-07) is internally consistent with the patch+numstat+files_changed; the patch applies cleanly via `git apply` against the base commit.
p2p_regression_note: test.sh uses bash hard-gates (no setup_dkms_linked.py / unparsable Python / no AMD source / script run rc!=0 -> 0.0; exit 0). No P2P_REGRESSION mechanism declared in test_manifest.yaml beyond the trailing comment line. Naturally bounded by `awk` clamp; no f2p_any_pass formula or additive-cap bug.
f2p_quality: GOOD — 7 weighted behavioral gates totaling 1.00 (idempotent re-run 0.10, symlink-tree breadth+integrity 0.15, essential source reachability 0.15, dkms.conf semantic correctness 0.20, Makefile content+parse 0.15, DKMS-style cp -L staging 0.15, cwd-independent symlinks 0.10). All implementation-agnostic; accepts both --force and idempotent re-run designs (F2P-1) and both amdgpu-dkms/<sub> and amdgpu-dkms/amd/<sub> tree layouts (F2P-2/3/6). Behavioral gates dominate; no AST/text-pattern overfitting.
validation_notes: Validated 2026-05-09 by sparse-cloning torvalds/linux at bc47b2e823914966c15a09422f8fc3aa98d34c1b, running test.sh with: (a) no script -> 0.0000 (hard gate); (b) no-op stub that only mkdirs amdgpu-dkms/ -> 0.1000 (F2P-1 only); (c) partial config-files-only solution -> 0.4500 (F2P-1, F2P-4, F2P-5); (d) canonical patch from data-pipeline/artifacts_unknown/canonical_patches/ses_372380598ffeG7W9GNRxrIR5np.json applied via `git apply` -> 1.0000 (all 7 F2P gates pass on Linux paths). Sharp baseline=0/canonical=1 discrimination confirmed.
